config.numberingShow.description: Whether to display multi-level title numbers in the outline view (e.g., 1.1, 1.2.1). **Requires reopening .do files**
config.numberingAdd.description: When numbering is enabled, automatically update section titles in .do files to include numbers. **Requires reopening .do files**
config.showRunButton.description: Whether to show the run button in the editor title bar.
config.stataPathOnWindows.description: **[Windows]** Path to Stata executable file (e.g., `C:\Program Files\Stata17\StataMP-64.exe`).
config.commentStyle.description: Comment style used for toggling comments. Options include `//`, `*`, or `/* ... */`
config.separatorLength.description: Total character length of the separator line (including the '** #' prefix and separators).
config.customCommands.description: User-defined Stata commands to highlight as keywords (array of strings). These commands will use the **same color** as built-in commands (like regress, summarize). Case-insensitive matching supported. Example: ["reghdfe", "ivreg2", "outreg2"]. **Requires reloading window** (Ctrl+Shift+P → Developer: Reload Window)
config.separatorSymmetric.description: Add ` **` at the end of separator lines to ensure visual symmetry (e.g., `** === title === **`).
config.stataStepDelayOnWindows.descripti: **[Windows]** Delay in milliseconds between each PowerShell command execution step. Increase this value if you experience issues on slower machines (default: 100ms, minimum: 50ms).
config.closeStataOtherWindowsBeforeSendi: **[Windows]** When running code, close Stata other windows (such as Viewer/Data Editor) before sending commands.
config.enableCtrlShiftD.description: Enable `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+D` as the run code shortcut (instead of the default `Ctrl/Cmd+D`).
config.cdToDoFileDir.description: Whether to automatically `cd` to the do file's directory when Stata is first launched. If disabled, Stata's working directory is not changed on startup.
config.runMode.description: Choose how Stata code is executed. `External App` sends code to the external Stata application installed on your system. `Embedded Console` runs code through the built-in Console | Stata All in One on the right side of VS Code, where you can view output and interact directly.
config.consoleFontMode.description: Choose the Embedded Console font source. `Editor Font Family` falls back to a cached system monospace font. `System Monospace` uses the cached platform monospace fallback directly. `Custom Font Family` gives your custom CSS font-family list highest priority, then falls back to the editor font and finally a cached system monospace font.
config.consoleCustomFontFamily.descripti: Custom CSS `font-family` list for the Embedded Console when `Console Font Source` is set to `Custom Font Family`. Example: `"Maple Mono NF CN", Menlo, Monaco, monospace`.
config.showActionButtons.description: Whether to show the Bug report and Sponsor buttons in the editor title bar.
